description: Persistent sadness, loss of interest, fatigue lasting 2+ weeks
symptoms: Persistent sadness, loss of interest, sleep disturbance, appetite changes, fatigue, worthlessness, difficulty concentrating, suicidal thoughts
risk_factors: Family history, trauma, chronic illness, substance abuse, stress, hormonal changes, medication side effects
treatment_options: Psychotherapy (CBT, IPT), Antidepressants (SSRIs), Lifestyle changes, Meditation, Exercise

description: Excessive worry about daily life lasting 6+ months
symptoms: Excessive worry, restlessness, fatigue, difficulty concentrating, irritability, muscle tension, sleep disturbance
risk_factors: Trauma, personality traits, medical conditions, caffeine, substance abuse, stress, genetic predisposition
treatment_options: Cognitive Behavioral Therapy, Medications (SSRIs, SNRIs), Relaxation techniques, Exercise, Meditation

description: Recurrent unexpected panic attacks with anticipatory anxiety
symptoms: Sudden intense fear, palpitations, sweating, trembling, shortness of breath, chest pain, dizziness, fear of dying
risk_factors: Stressful life events, family history, caffeine sensitivity, medical conditions, substance use
treatment_options: Cognitive Behavioral Therapy, Exposure therapy, Medications, Breathing techniques, Lifestyle changes

description: Persistent distress following exposure to traumatic event
symptoms: Intrusive memories, nightmares, avoidance, negative thoughts, increased arousal, emotional numbness, hypervigilance
risk_factors: Exposure to trauma, lack of social support, previous mental health issues, substance abuse, genetic factors
treatment_options: Trauma-focused CBT, EMDR, Medications, Support groups, Mindfulness

description: Intense fear of social situations and performance
symptoms: Extreme fear of social judgment, avoidance of social situations, physical symptoms (blushing, sweating, trembling), intense distress in social settings
risk_factors: Temperament, trauma, family history, social stress, media influence
treatment_options: Cognitive Behavioral Therapy, Group therapy, Exposure therapy, Medications, Social skills training

description: Alternating periods of mania/hypomania and depression
symptoms: Periods of extreme happiness/irritability (mania), increased energy, racing thoughts, impulsive behavior, followed by periods of severe depression
risk_factors: Family history, brain chemistry changes, stress, substance abuse, sleep disruption
treatment_options: Mood stabilizers (lithium), Antipsychotics, Psychotherapy, Lifestyle management, Sleep hygiene

description: Recurrent obsessions and compulsions interfering with functioning
symptoms: Intrusive unwanted thoughts, need for control/symmetry, contamination fears, compulsive behaviors, anxiety when unable to perform rituals
risk_factors: Stress, perfectionism, family history, brain chemistry, trauma
treatment_options: Cognitive Behavioral Therapy, Exposure and Response Prevention (ERP), Medications (SSRIs), Family therapy

description: Persistent pattern of inattention and/or hyperactivity
symptoms: Difficulty sustaining attention, forgetfulness, easily distracted, impulsivity, restlessness, difficulty organizing, talking excessively, interrupting
risk_factors: Genetic factors, prenatal factors, neurochemistry imbalances, environmental stressors, lead exposure
treatment_options: Medications (stimulants, non-stimulants), Behavioral therapy, Cognitive Behavioral Therapy, Organizational strategies, Exercise

description: Severe disturbance in eating behaviors and related thoughts
symptoms: Restrictive eating, binge eating, purging behaviors, excessive exercise, preoccupation with weight, distorted body image, social withdrawal
risk_factors: Perfectionism, pressure to be thin, trauma, family dysfunction, media influence, biological factors
treatment_options: Cognitive Behavioral Therapy, Family therapy, Nutritional counseling, Medications, Inpatient treatment if severe

description: Problematic pattern of substance use causing significant impairment
symptoms: Loss of control, continued use despite consequences, neglect of responsibilities, social withdrawal, tolerance, withdrawal symptoms, risky behavior
risk_factors: Genetics, trauma, stress, peer pressure, mental health conditions, availability of substances
treatment_options: 12-step programs, Cognitive Behavioral Therapy, Medication-assisted treatment, Peer support, Residential treatment
